rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN M. PICETTI 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, First Accounting of Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) Notice to Ken Marks II was not given to his place of residence or business (street address instead of P.O. Box). Probate Code section 1215(a)(3). 2) Notice must also be given to Mark Clausen as the attorney of record for Ken Marks II. 3) A statement of each financial account covering a period including 06/30/2023 must be filed. LRSF 14.34(D). 4) A declaration re retention of original financial account statements and compliance with inspection thereof required by LRSF 14.59(A) and CRC 2.257 must be filed. 5) Although the Court never set a status hearing on the filing of petitioner's first account as successor trustee, petitioner was required to file his first account "at the expiration of one year from the time of appointment", as required by section 2620(a) (applicable to court-supervised trusts under LRSF 14.47(B)(5)). While the former trustee's final account was not settled until June 2023, no explanation has been provided as to why petitioner's first account was not filed more than 2 years later in August 2025. According to the attorneys' supporting time records, "petition for approval of first accounting, trustee fees, and attorney fees" was prepared and revised in September 2022. 6) The petition did not address sufficiency of bond. LRSF 14.34(E). 7) The beginning carry value of each security held in the Schwab brokerage account is different from the ending carry value reported in the former trustee's final account. If petitioner elected to reappraise each security as of the date of his appointment, petitioner was required to file an inventory and appraisal after requesting appointment of a probate referee. 8) The assets on hand at the beginning of this account added $150,310 past due rent as a new asset (which was not reported and transferred by the former trustee), but no inventory and appraisal has been filed to support its beginning carry value. 9) The beginning date of this account predates the ending date of the former trustee's final account, but most transactions that occurred between those dates were not reported. The assets on hand at the beginning of this account period included all trust assets, even though the former trustee did not actually deliver any asset to petitioner until 07/30/2021 (and the trust securities were not transferred until 09/01/2021). The beginning balance of this account should be $0, and each asset should be listed on the schedule of additional property received so that the transactions performed by the former trustee prior to delivery of each asset need not be included in this account. 10) Receipts and disbursements during the first year of this account were not allocated between principal and income. CRC 7.901(c). 11) Receipts and disbursements must be reported in subject-matter categories, with each category subtotaled on a separate form. CRC 7.575(c). 12) Was prior court approval obtained re $10,000 legal fees paid to Macinnis, Donner & Koplowitz on 06/30/2022? 13) Each "accrued interest paid" during the first year of this account must be reported as a negative receipt, rather than as a disbursement. See CEB Fiduciary Accounting Handbook section 1.7.4. 14) Each "reimbursement" listed on the schedules of disbursements and distributions must disclose the original payee, including $120,000 "legal fees" reimbursed to the settlor between July 2022 and June 2023. LRSF 14.34(B). Also, was prior court approval obtained for payment of those legal fees? 15) There was no explanation re the nature of "miscellaneous income adjustment" reported as a $49.44 receipt on 06/30/2023. Was this item added as a receipt to balance the account?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LADD KUBES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. Grant without hearing, although this Court does not ordinarily allow costs associated with legal research, such as Westlaw, and the petition caption and some of the relief sought did not make sense when the executor, as the sole beneficiary, waived an accounting. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate an estate proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.
